title: भगवद्गीता अध्याय १५ - पुरुषोत्तमयोग
devanagariText:
॥ अथ पञ्चदशोऽध्यायः - पुरुषोत्तमयोगः ॥

श्रीभगवानुवाच —
ऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम्।
छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित्॥१॥

अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा
गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः।
अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि
कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके॥२॥

न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते
नान्तो न चादिर्न च सम्प्रतिष्ठा।
अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूलम्
असङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा॥३॥

ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्यं
यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः।
तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये
यतः प्रवृत्तिः प्रसृता पुराणी॥४॥

निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा
अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः।
द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञैर्
गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत्॥५॥

न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः।
यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम॥६॥

ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः।
मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति॥७॥

शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः।
गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात्॥८॥

श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च।
अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते॥९॥

उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वापि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम्।
विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुषः॥१०॥

यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम्।
यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः॥११॥

यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम्।
यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम्॥१२॥

गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा।
पुष्णामि चौषधीः सर्वाः सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मकः॥१३॥

अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रितः।
प्राणापानसमायुक्तः पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम्॥१४॥

सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो
मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च।
वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो
वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम्॥१५॥

द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च।
क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते॥१६॥

उत्तमः पुरुषस्त्वन्यः परमात्मेत्युदाहृतः।
यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वरः॥१७॥

यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तमः।
अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथितः पुरुषोत्तमः॥१८॥

यो मामेवमसम्मूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम्।
स सर्वविद्भजति मां सर्वभावेन भारत॥१९॥

इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयानघ।
एतद्बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत॥२०॥

॥ इति श्रीमद्भगवद्गीतासूपनिषत्सु ब्रह्मविद्यायां योगशास्त्रे
श्रीकृष्णार्जुनसंवादे पुरुषोत्तमयोगो नाम पञ्चदशोऽध्यायः ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Panchadasho'dhyayah - Purushottama Yogah ||

Shri Bhagavan Uvacha —
Urdhva-Mulam Adhah-Shakham-Ashvattham Prahur-Avyayam.
Chhandamsi Yasya Parnani Yas-Tam Veda Sa Veda-Vit.||1||

Adhash-Chordhvam Prasritas-Tasya Shakha
Guna-Pravruddha Vishaya-Pravalah.
Adhash-Cha Mulany-Anusantatani
Karma-Anubandhini Manushya-Loke.||2||

Na Rupam-Asye-Ha Tatho-Palabdhyate
Na-Anto Na Cha-Adir-Na Cha Sampratishtha.
Ashvattham-Enam Suvi-Rudha-Mulam
Asanga-Shastrena Dridhena Chhittva.||3||

Tatah Padam Tat-Parimargitavyam
Yasmin Gata Na Nivartanti Bhuyah.
Tam-Eva Cha-Adyam Purusham Prapadye
Yatah Pravrittih Prasrita Purani.||4||

Na Tad-Bhasayate Suryo Na Shashanko Na Pavakah.
Yad-Gatva Na Nivartante Tad-Dhama Paramam Mama.||6||

Mama-Eva-Amsho Jiva-Loke Jiva-Bhutah Sanatanah.
Manah-Shashtani-Indriyani Prakriti-Sthani Karshati.||7||

Aham Vaishvanaro Bhutva Praninam Deham-Ashritah.
Prana-Apana-Samayuktah Pachamy-Annam Chatur-Vidham.||14||

Sarvasya Cha-Aham Hridi Sannivishtho
Mattah Smritir-Jnanam-Apohanam Cha.
Vedaish-Cha Sarvair-Aham-Eva Vedyo
Vedanta-Krid-Veda-Vid-Eva Cha-Aham.||15||

Dvav-Imau Purushau Loke Ksharash-Cha-Akshara Eva Cha.
Ksharah Sarvani Bhutani Kutastho'kshara Uchyate.||16||

Uttamah Purushas-Tv-Anyah Parama-Atme-Ty-Udahritah.
Yo Loka-Trayam-Avishya Bibharty-Avyaya Ishvarah.||17||

Yasmat-Ksharam-Atito'ham-Aksharad-Api Cho-Uttamah.
Ato'smi Loke Vede Cha Prathitah Purushottamah.||18||

Yo Mam-Evam-Asammudho Janati Purushottamam.
Sa Sarva-Vid-Bhajati Mam Sarva-Bhavena Bharata.||19||

Iti Guhyatamam Shastram-Idam-Uktam Maya-Anagha.
Etad-Buddhva Buddhiman-Syat Krita-Krityash-Cha Bharata.||20||

|| Iti Shrimad-Bhagavad-Gitasu-Upanishatsu Brahma-Vidyayam Yoga-Shastre
Shri-Krishna-Arjuna-Samvade Purushottama-Yogo Nama Panchadasho'dhyayah ||
hindiMeaning: पञ्चदश अध्याय 'पुरुषोत्तमयोग' गीता का अत्यंत गहन अध्याय है। कृष्ण संसार को उलटे वृक्ष (अश्वत्थ) की उपमा देते हैं — जिसकी जड़ें ऊपर (ब्रह्म में) और शाखाएँ नीचे हैं। इस वृक्ष को वैराग्य रूपी शस्त्र से काटकर उस परम पद की खोज करनी चाहिए जहाँ से लौटना नहीं होता। जहाँ न सूर्य प्रकाशित करता है, न चन्द्रमा, न अग्नि — वह मेरा परम धाम है। जीव मेरा ही सनातन अंश है। मैं वैश्वानर अग्नि बनकर प्राणियों के शरीर में चार प्रकार के अन्न पचाता हूँ। सबके हृदय में स्थित हूँ, मुझसे स्मृति, ज्ञान और अपोहन (विस्मरण) होता है। सब वेदों से मैं ही जानने योग्य हूँ। क्षर (नाशवान जीव), अक्षर (अविनाशी कूटस्थ) और पुरुषोत्तम (परमात्मा) — ये तीन पुरुष हैं। मैं क्षर और अक्षर दोनों से उत्तम हूँ, इसलिए 'पुरुषोत्तम' कहलाता हूँ।
benefits: Contains the complete Ashvattha Vriksha (cosmic tree) allegory
Reveals Krishna as Purushottama — the Supreme Person beyond all
Teaches that the soul is an eternal fragment of the Divine
Explains how God sustains all life as the digestive fire
Contains the key verse: Sarvasya Chaham Hridi Sannivishtho
Short but profound — called the most secret teaching by Krishna himself
Traditionally recited daily for spiritual advancement
description: The Bhagavad Gita Chapter 15 - Purushottama Yoga, known in Sanskrit as भगवद्गीता अध्याय १५ - पुरुषोत्तमयोग, is a sacred prayer in praise of Lord Krishna. Rooted in Bhagavad Gita - Mahabharata, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to contain the key verse: Sarvasya Chaham Hridi Sannivishtho, traditionally recited daily for spiritual advancement, and contain the complete Ashvattha Vriksha (cosmic tree) allegory. This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami to invoke divine grace. With 20 verses and a reading time of about 10 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Bhagavad Gita Chapter 15 - Purushottama Yoga in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Bhagavad Gita Chapter 15 Purushottama Yoga in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. 20 verses on the Supreme Person and cosmic tree.

seoFaqs.question: What is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 15 — Purushottama Yoga?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 15 (भगवद्गीता अध्याय १५), titled Purushottama Yoga (the Yoga of the Supreme Person), is the fifteenth chapter of the Srimad Bhagavad Gita and one of its most beloved short chapters — only 20 verses. The chapter opens with the magnificent metaphor of the inverted Ashvattha tree (the cosmic peepal tree of samsara with roots upwards in Brahman and branches downwards as the worlds), teaches the necessity of cutting this tree of attachment with the axe of detachment, and then declares the Lord as the Purushottama — the Supreme Person who transcends both the kshara (the perishable individual selves) and the akshara (the imperishable unmanifest). The chapter culminates in the celebrated declaration that the Lord is seated in the heart of every being (Sarvasya chaham hridi sannivishto). (Source: Srimad Bhagavad Gita Chapter 15; Mahabharata Bhishma Parva.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the inverted Ashvattha tree allegory in Chapter 15?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 15.1-15.4 give the famous Ashvattha-vriksha allegory: Urdhva-mulam adhah-shakham ashvattham prahur avyayam — They speak of the imperishable peepal tree with roots above and branches below; whose leaves are the Vedic hymns; he who knows it is the knower of the Veda. Its branches extend downwards and upwards nourished by the gunas, with sense-objects as buds; its roots also extend downwards in the world of men (binding through karma). The form of this tree cannot be perceived here as it is — neither its end, its beginning, nor its support. Having cut this firmly-rooted Ashvattha with the strong axe of detachment (asanga-shastrena dridhena chittva), one should then seek that goal from which there is no return — taking refuge in that primal Person from whom the ancient activity has streamed forth. The tree symbolises samsara — beginningless, ever-growing, sustained by desires and karma; only vairagya (dispassion) can fell it. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 15.1-15.4; cf. Katha Upanishad 6.1.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the famous verse Mamaivamsho Jivaloke mean in Chapter 15?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 15.7 — Mamaivamsho jiva-loke jiva-bhutah sanatanah, manah-shashthani-indriyani prakriti-sthani karshati — A fragment of My very Self, having become an individual jiva in the world of beings, is eternal; he attracts the senses, of which the mind is the sixth, that abide in prakriti. This is one of the most theologically debated verses in Vedanta. Sri Vaishnavas (Ramanuja) read amsha as the soul being a real eternal part of the Lord. Madhvas read it as a permanent eternal distinction. Advaitins (Shankara) read amsha as a reflection (abhasa) — the jiva is Brahman in essence, only apparently fragmented by the upadhis (limiting adjuncts) of mind and senses. The verse establishes that every individual self is intimately and eternally related to the Supreme — the foundation of all bhakti and the ultimate basis for liberation. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 15.7; Sri Bhashya; Brahma Sutra Bhashyas.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 15 traditionally recited before meals?
seoFaqs.answer: Chapter 15 is the supreme bhojan-paryanta paath (recitation accompanying food) in classical Hindu tradition because verse 15.14 explicitly identifies the Lord with the digestive fire: Aham vaishvanaro bhutva praninam dehamashritah, pranapana-samayuktah pachamy-annam chatur-vidham — Becoming the Vaishvanara fire, having entered the bodies of all living beings, joined with the prana and apana airs, I digest the four kinds of food (chewable, suckable, lickable, drinkable). By reciting Chapter 15 before eating, the devotee surrenders the meal to the Vaishvanara within. Many Hindu families recite the entire Chapter 15 (or at least verses 14-15) before lunch and dinner, often combined with the Bhojan-mantra Brahmarpanam Brahma havir (Bhagavad Gita 4.24). The Pushtimarga, Madhva and Smarta traditions especially emphasise this practice. (Sources: Bhagavad Gita 15.14; classical Hindu bhojan-vidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Sarvasya chaham hridi sannivishto in Chapter 15?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 15.15 declares: Sarvasya chaham hridi sannivishto, mattah smritir-jnanam-apohanam cha, vedaish cha sarvair aham eva vedyo, vedanta-krid veda-vid eva chaham — I am seated in the heart of all; from me are memory, knowledge and their loss; I alone am the object of all the Vedas; I am the maker of the Vedanta and I am the knower of the Vedas. This verse establishes (1) the antaryamin doctrine — the Lord as the inner controller seated in the heart, (2) the Lord as the source of all cognition, recall and forgetting, (3) the Lord as the singular goal of all Vedic teaching, and (4) the Lord as the author of the Vedanta-darshana itself. Acharyas across schools quote this verse as one of the central pramanas (proof-texts) of the Gita. It is the principal verse for daily Krishna-meditation. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 15.15.)

seoFaqs.question: What are Kshara, Akshara and Purushottama in Chapter 15?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 15.16-15.19 give the three-Purusha doctrine: Dvau-imau purushau loke ksharash-cha-akshara eva cha — Two purushas exist in the world, the perishable and the imperishable. The kshara purusha consists of all manifest beings (sarvani bhutani) — the entire stream of jivas in the cosmos. The akshara is the kutastha — the immutable unmanifest Brahman. But beyond both is the supreme Purusha: Uttamah purushas-tv-anyah paramatmety-udahritah, yo loka-trayam-avishya bibharty-avyaya ishvarah — But the Supreme Person is another, called the Paramatman, the imperishable Lord who, having entered the three worlds, sustains them. Verse 15.18 declares: Yasmat ksharam atito'ham, aksharad-api chottamah, ato'smi loke vede cha prathitah purushottamah — Because I transcend the perishable and am higher than the imperishable, I am celebrated in the world and the Veda as Purushottama. This three-Purusha schema is the principal contribution of Chapter 15 to Vedanta. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 15.16-15.19.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 15 traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Chapter 15 is recited on Day 15 of an 18-day Gita Parayana cycle, but uniquely is recited daily before meals in many Hindu households (because of verse 15.14 — the Vaishvanara teaching). Specific occasions include: Wednesday (Budhvar — Krishna's day), every meal, Purushottama Ekadashi (the Ekadashi of Adhik Maas — the intercalary lunar month named after Purushottama Krishna himself), Adhik Maas (Purushottama Maas — the entire month is dedicated to Purushottama), Gita Jayanti (Margashirsha Shukla Ekadashi), and Janmashtami. The chapter is also chanted by departing souls and at the bedside of the dying — because it teaches the supreme refuge in Purushottama and is the shortest chapter that contains the complete Vedanta. Many sadhakas pair Chapter 12 (Bhakti) and Chapter 15 (Purushottama) as their daily two-chapter parayana. (Sources: Gita Mahatmya; Padma Purana Adhik Maas Mahatmya; Bhagavad Gita 15.14.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Chapter 15 connect to the verse-by-verse Gita pages on this site?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 15 — Purushottama Yoga contains 20 verses, each studied verse-by-verse on this site at /gita/chapter-15/verse-1 through /gita/chapter-15/verse-20. The verse-level pages provide the Devanagari, transliteration, word-by-word meaning, English translation, Hindi anvaya and commentary by classical acharyas (Shankara, Ramanuja, Madhva) for each shloka — particularly important for the philosophically dense verses on the Ashvattha tree (15.1-4), Mamaivamsho (15.7), Vaishvanara (15.14), Sarvasya (15.15), and the three Purushas (15.16-19). This stotra page presents the entire chapter as a single text for the very popular daily before-meals parayana of Purushottama Yoga. Many Hindu families memorise Chapter 15 before any other Gita chapter. (Source: Stotra and Gita parayana traditions.)
